Common Cold.description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract that primarily affects the nose and throat. Usually mild and self-limiting, lasting 7-10 days.
precautions: Get plenty of rest and sleep
Drink warm fluids like soup and herbal tea
Use saline nasal sprays to relieve congestion
Wash hands frequently to prevent spread

Influenza (Flu).description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses. More severe than the common cold and can lead to serious complications.
precautions: Rest and stay hydrated with plenty of fluids
Take antiviral medication if prescribed within 48 hours
Isolate from others to prevent transmission
Consult a doctor if symptoms worsen

COVID-19.description: A respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus. Symptoms range from mild to severe and may include long-term effects.
precautions: Isolate immediately and get a PCR or antigen test
Wear a mask around others in your household
Monitor oxygen saturation with a pulse oximeter
Seek emergency care if breathing becomes difficult

Pneumonia.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid. Can be caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi.
precautions: Take prescribed antibiotics on schedule and complete the course
Use a humidifier and drink warm liquids
Get adequate rest to help recovery
Seek immediate medical care for severe breathing difficulty

Bronchitis.description: An inflammation of the lining of the bronchial tubes that carry air to and from the lungs. Often follows a cold or flu.
precautions: Avoid smoking and exposure to lung irritants
Use a humidifier to add moisture to the air
Drink fluids to thin mucus
Take cough medicine only if cough disrupts sleep

Asthma.description: A chronic condition where airways become inflamed, narrow, and produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.
precautions: Use prescribed inhalers as directed by your doctor
Identify and avoid known triggers like dust or pollen
Follow your asthma action plan
Keep rescue inhaler accessible at all times

Allergic Rhinitis.description: An allergic response to airborne particles like pollen, dust mites, or pet dander, causing cold-like symptoms.
precautions: Take antihistamines as recommended
Avoid known allergens when possible
Keep windows closed during high pollen seasons
Use HEPA air filters indoors

Migraine.description: A neurological condition characterized by intense, debilitating headaches, often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light and sound.
precautions: Rest in a dark, quiet room during episodes
Apply cold compress to forehead
Take prescribed migraine medication early
Identify and avoid personal triggers

Tension Headache.description: The most common type of headache, often described as a dull, aching pain or pressure around the forehead, sides, and back of the head.
precautions: Practice stress management techniques
Apply heat or cold to neck and shoulders
Maintain proper posture
Get adequate sleep and stay hydrated

Gastroenteritis.description: An intestinal infection causing inflammation of the stomach and intestines, commonly known as the stomach flu.
precautions: Stay hydrated with clear fluids and electrolyte drinks
Eat bland foods like toast, rice, and bananas (BRAT diet)
Avoid dairy, caffeine, and fatty foods
Wash hands thoroughly to prevent spread

Food Poisoning.description: Illness caused by consuming contaminated food, resulting from bacteria, viruses, parasites, or their toxins.
precautions: Drink plenty of water to prevent dehydration
Rest and avoid solid food until vomiting stops
Gradually reintroduce bland foods
Seek medical care if symptoms persist over 48 hours

Diabetes.description: A chronic metabolic disease characterized by elevated blood glucose levels, requiring lifelong management.
precautions: Monitor blood glucose levels regularly
Follow a balanced, low-sugar diet
Exercise regularly as recommended by your doctor
Take prescribed medications consistently

Hypertension.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure that can lead to serious health problems including heart disease and stroke.
precautions: Reduce sodium intake in your diet
Exercise regularly with cardio activities
Limit alcohol and avoid tobacco
Take prescribed antihypertensive medications

Anemia.description: A condition where the body lacks enough healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to tissues.
precautions: Eat iron-rich foods like spinach, red meat, and beans
Take iron supplements if prescribed
Pair iron-rich foods with vitamin C for absorption
Get regular blood tests to monitor levels

Malaria.description: A serious mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites, common in tropical and subtropical regions.
precautions: Take antimalarial medication as prescribed
Use mosquito nets and insect repellent
Wear long-sleeved clothing in endemic areas
Seek prompt medical evaluation for fever

Dengue Fever.description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing flu-like illness, with severe forms potentially life-threatening.
precautions: Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions
Take acetaminophen for fever (avoid aspirin/ibuprofen)
Get plenty of rest
Monitor platelet count with blood tests

Typhoid.description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water.
precautions: Complete the full course of prescribed antibiotics
Drink only boiled or bottled water
Maintain strict hand hygiene
Avoid raw vegetables and unpeeled fruits

Chickenpox.description: A highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy, blister-like rash on the skin, most common in children.
precautions: Isolate to prevent spreading to others
Apply calamine lotion to relieve itching
Keep fingernails short to avoid scratching
Take cool baths with baking soda

Urinary Tract Infection.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system, most commonly affecting the bladder and urethra.
precautions: Drink plenty of water to flush bacteria
Complete the full course of antibiotics
Urinate frequently and after intercourse
Wipe from front to back to prevent infection

Acid Reflux.description: A digestive disorder where stomach acid frequently flows back into the esophagus, causing heartburn and irritation.
precautions: Avoid spicy, fatty, and acidic foods
Eat smaller meals and avoid lying down after eating
Elevate the head of your bed
Take antacids or prescribed medications

Anxiety Disorder.description: A mental health condition characterized by persistent, excessive worry that interferes with daily activities.
precautions: Practice deep breathing and mindfulness exercises
Limit caffeine and alcohol intake
Maintain regular exercise and sleep schedule
Consider therapy or counseling support

Hepatitis.description: Inflammation of the liver, commonly caused by viral infections (Hepatitis A, B, C, D, or E) or other factors.
precautions: Avoid alcohol completely
Get vaccinated for Hepatitis A and B
Practice safe food and water hygiene
Follow antiviral treatment if prescribed

Tuberculosis.description: A potentially serious infectious bacterial disease that mainly affects the lungs, spread through the air.
precautions: Take all TB medications for the full duration (6+ months)
Cover mouth when coughing or sneezing
Ensure good ventilation in living spaces
Get regular follow-up tests
